input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Implement strict firewall rules on the Fortigate firewall to limit network scanning techniques and restrict unnecessary inbound and lateral traffic, especially to critical devices like routers and switches.", "Deploy network segmentation to isolate critical network segments, reducing the attack surface and limiting the UbuntuComputer's ability to perform reconnaissance against other subnets.", "Utilize and configure fail2ban on the UbuntuComputer to detect and block repeated scanning attempts or suspicious connection behaviors originating from the compromised host."], "chosen_mitigation": "Implement strict firewall rules on the Fortigate firewall to limit network scanning techniques and restrict unnecessary inbound and lateral traffic, especially to critical devices like routers and switches.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false}

{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Implement strict firewall rules on the Fortigate firewall to limit network scanning techniques and restrict unnecessary inbound and lateral traffic, especially to critical devices like routers and switches.",
  "comments": "Configured the Fortigate firewall to create a custom address object for the target router IP 192.168.1.1 and a custom service for the scanning ports (22, 80, 443). Then added multiple firewall policies to deny traffic from port1 to port2, port2 to port3, and port2 to port1 specifically for the scanning ports targeting the router IP. This effectively limits the scanning attempts by blocking and logging scan traffic, mitigating reconnaissance and attack success while the attacker is still active in the network.",
  "device_identifier": "fortigate-firewall",
  "commands": [
    "config firewall address
edit "target-router-192.168.1.1"
set subnet 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.255
next
end",
    "config firewall service custom
edit "nmap-ports"
set tcp-portrange 22 22
set tcp-portrange 80 80
set tcp-portrange 443 443
next
end",
    "config firewall policy
edit 1
set name "block-nmap-scan"
set srcintf "port1"
set dstintf "port2"
set srcaddr all
set dstaddr "target-router-192.168.1.1"
set action deny
set schedule always
set service "nmap-ports"
set logtraffic all
next
edit 2
set name "block-nmap-scan-2"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port3"
set srcaddr all
set dstaddr "target-router-192.168.1.1"
set action deny
set schedule always
set service "nmap-ports"
set logtraffic all
next
edit 3
set name "block-nmap-scan-3"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr all
set dstaddr "target-router-192.168.1.1"
set action deny
set schedule always
set service "nmap-ports"
set logtraffic all
next
end"
  ]
}
